title: Tiko and the Glowing Moss Cradle
description: In a forest where fireflies hum lullabies, a tiny tree frog discovers patches of moss that glow like moonlight. As twilight deepens, he weaves them into a cradle, not for himself—but for the forest’s smallest creatures to rest.
text:
[HUMMING]

Lu mi mi, lu mi mi,
Ba doo na, ba doo na,
Hoo wa ni, hoo wa ni,

Lu mi mi, lu mi mi,
Glow so warm, glow so soft,
Ba doo na, ba doo na,

[GENTLE]

Mm wa ni, mm wa ni,
Rest now, little one,
Hoo la lu, hoo la lu,

Mm wa ni, mm wa ni,
Dream with the moss,
Lu mi mi, lu mi mi,

[SLEEPY]

Shh la lu, shh la lu,
Moonlight holds you,
Mm...

morals: Kindness grows when we create space for others.
Even the smallest hands can build something beautiful.

character.name: Tiko
character.identity: A gentle tree frog who tends to the forest’s quiet needs
character.special: He can coax moss to glow brighter with his touch

cover_context: A twilight forest floor dappled in soft blue-green bioluminescent moss, fireflies drifting like tiny lanterns, dew-kissed ferns curling around a woven cradle of glowing vines.
